description: SpyCatcher — 116-aa protein partner (engineered from the CnaB2 domain of S. pyogenes FbaB) that forms a spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide bond to its cognate peptide SpyTag.

label: SpyCatcher
note: 116-aa protein partner of the SpyTag/SpyCatcher covalent-conjugation system, engineered by splitting the CnaB2 immunoglobulin-like collagen-adhesion domain of Streptococcus pyogenes fibronectin-binding protein FbaB into a protein fragment (SpyCatcher, contains the reactive Lys31) and a 13-aa peptide (SpyTag, contains the reactive Asp). On mixing with SpyTag, the side-chain amine of SpyCatcher Lys31 spontaneously and irreversibly forms an intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond to SpyTag Asp7, catalysed by SpyCatcher Glu77; the linkage resists boiling in SDS and forces up to ~1 nN. As stored the domain begins with a vector-derived Gly-Ala-Met (residues 24-139 of AFD50637.1, the standard post-TEV-cleavage product) and is fusable at a protein N- or C-terminus. Structural and biochemical work shows the N-terminal ~23 and C-terminal ~9 residues are dispensable, defining a minimized 84-aa core (original residues 21-104).

provenance.sequence_source: JQ478411.1 (Zakeri et al. PNAS 2012, synthetic construct SpyCatcher gene complete cds), CDS translation protein_id AFD50637.1, residues 24-139 (post-TEV cleavage of N-terminal His6+TEV tag MSYYHHHHHHDYDIPTTENLYFQ), 100% identity over 116 aa; independently fetched and compared 2026-06-22

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous, irreversible isopeptide (amide) bond from SpyCatcher Lys31 to SpyTag Asp7, catalysed by Glu77

source.quote: The electron density map clearly confirms the isopeptide bond between Lys31 of SpyCatcher and Asp7 of SpyTag
source.section: Crystal structure of the SpyTag/SpyCatcher complex

functional_claims.label: SpyCatcher + SpyTag form a compact beta-sandwich (reformed CnaB2 fold); structure solved at 2.1 A (PDB 4MLI)

value.hydrophobic_pocket: Phe29/75/92, Ile27/90, Met44 of SpyCatcher receive Ile3/Met5 of SpyTag
value.related_structure: PDB 4MLS (SpyCatcherDeltaN1/SpyTag, 2.0 A, r.m.s.d. 0.88 A to full-length complex)

source.quote: SpyCatcher and SpyTag together form a compact β-sandwich structure ... The final structure was indexed to the P1 space group and was refined to a resolution of 2.1 Å ... Coordinates and structure factors have been deposited in the Protein Data Bank (PDB 4MLI).
source.section: Crystal structure of the SpyTag/SpyCatcher complex

functional_claims.label: N-terminal ~23 and C-terminal ~9 residues are disordered/dispensable; minimal SpyCatcher is residues 21-104 (84 aa)

value.n_terminal_dispensable: first ~23 residues (disordered in crystal; deletable as SpyCatcherDeltaN1/N2/N3)
value.c_terminal_dispensable: last ~9 residues (backbone traceable only to residue 103; C-terminal 10 disordered)
value.effect_of_minimization: comparable reaction efficiency to full-length SpyCatcher

source.quote: our structural and biochemical data indicate that a minimal SpyTag/SpyCatcher system consists of residues 21–104 of the original SpyCatcher. The minimization of SpyCatcher from 116 amino acids to a core 84 amino acid construct should enhance its applicability as a fusion tag
source.section: The N-terminal and C-terminal segments of SpyCatcher are not required for efficient reaction with SpyTag

functional_claims.label: Reacts with SpyTag with a half-time of 74 s at 10 uM (second-order, original pair)

value.reaction: covalent isopeptide bond formation with SpyTag
value.reconstitution_kinetics: ~40% complex after 1 min, ~65% after 10 min (SpyCatcher + SpyTag-MBP)

source.quote: The two partners then react rapidly, forming the isopeptide bond, with a half-time of 74 s for partners at 10 μM.

functional_claims.label: Reaction is robust across pH, temperature and buffer; complex resists boiling in SDS

value.stability: covalent complex not reversed by boiling in SDS or competing peptide; bond breaks only above ~1 nN force

source.quote: Reaction occurred in high yield simply upon mixing and amidst diverse conditions of pH, temperature, and buffer ... Peptide binding was not reversed by boiling or competing peptide. Single-molecule dynamic force spectroscopy showed that SpyTag did not separate from SpyCatcher until the force exceeded 1 nN, where covalent bonds snap.

functional_claims.label: Used as an N- or C-terminal fusion partner; reacts with SpyTag placed at either terminus or internally

value.partner_placement: SpyTag fusable N-terminally, C-terminally, or internally
value.stored_form: domain prefixed with vector-derived Gly-Ala-Met (post-TEV product)

source.quote: SpyTag could be fused at either terminus or internally and reacted specifically at the mammalian cell surface.

functional_claims.label: Functional as recombinant fusions in bacteria and displayed/reactive at the mammalian cell surface

source.quote: Plasma membrane display of the construct was tested ... We found similar strong cell surface expression of SpyCatcher, SpyCatcher003, and SpyCatcher003 EA

functional_claims.label: Engineered from the CnaB2 domain of S. pyogenes FbaB; the protein fragment retaining the reactive Lys31

value.design: split of CnaB2 into a protein fragment (SpyCatcher, Lys31) and a peptide (SpyTag, Asp) plus rational engineering
value.sequence_record: AFD50637.1 / JQ478411.1, residues 24-139 (post-TEV product, N-terminal Gly-Ala-Met)

source.quote: fibronectin-binding protein FbaB contains a domain with a spontaneous isopeptide bond between Lys and Asp. By splitting this domain and rational engineering of the fragments, we obtained a peptide (SpyTag) which formed an amide bond to its protein partner (SpyCatcher)

functional_claims.label: Faster-reacting descendants SpyCatcher002 and SpyCatcher003; E77A is an inactive control

variants.name: SpyCatcher003
variants.mutations: A89P, T91E, Q97D, N103D, K108E (relative to SpyCatcher002 lineage)
variants.note: approaches the diffusion limit; back-compatible with earlier SpyTag/SpyCatcher generations

variants.name: SpyCatcher E77A
variants.note: single-residue catalytic knockout; abolishes isopeptide-bond formation (specificity / negative control)

source.quote: we made 4 mutations (T91E, Q97D, N103D, and K108E) from SpyCatcher002 ... to give the final SpyCatcher003 ... The second-order rate constant for SpyTag003/SpyCatcher003 was 5.5 ± 0.6 × 10[5] M[-1]s[-1] ... ∼400-fold faster than the 1.4 × 10[3] M[-1]s[-1] previously shown for SpyTag/SpyCatcher

functional_claims.label: Genetically encoded covalent module for irreversible protein conjugation, immobilization, vaccines and biomaterials

applications: modular protein architectures, hydrogels and biomaterials
VLP / outer-membrane-vesicle antigen display for vaccines
cell-surface and bacterial-protein labelling and imaging

source.quote: Review of SpyCatcher/SpyTag applications (OMVs, VLPs, hydrogels, imaging)
